subject: [PATCH v2] nfsd: recall deleg if a requested dir attr change can't be encoded

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch does not fully address the issue of silently masking genuine encode failures and vfs_getattr failures as 0-length attributes, which causes directory attribute change events to be omitted instead of triggering a delegation recall.
sentiment_signals: patch is not yet a complete fix
reviews.raw_body:
Sashiko reports (and gpt-5.5 agrees):

Pre-existing issues:
- [High] Genuine encode failures and `vfs_getattr` failures are silently masked as 0-length attributes, causing the directory attribute change event to be erroneously omitted instead of triggering a delegation recall.

But this isn’t really a pre-existing issue. It pre-exists this fix, yes,
but it was introduced by “nfsd: add support to CB_NOTIFY for dir
attribute changes”. What that means is this patch is not yet a complete
fix.


-- 
Chuck Lever

2026-06-19.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the NFSv4 server where it silently drops directory attribute changes when they cannot be encoded, leading to stale cached directory attributes on the client side. The patch recalls the delegation when such an error occurs, as required by RFC 8881. It also distinguishes between genuine encode failures and benign cases where no specific dir attributes are requested.
